title: thuja occidentalis f. aurea
intro: Thuja occidentalis f. aurea, also known as golden arborvitae, is a cultivar of the native North American evergreen tree Thuja occidentalis, characterized by its golden-yellow foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to its essential oils and terpenes. Thuja occidentalis f. aurea has been used to treat a range of health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems, as well as to reduce fever and relieve pain. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through various herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically or ingested to reap its health benefits.
